content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS of length nnn and an integer KKK.

We will define F(L)F(L)F(L) here. Let's choose KKK random sub-strings of length LLL from SSS.

Then F(L)F(L)F(L) equals the probability such that all of them are pairwise identical.

See the **Explanation** section for more clarity.

You have to find F(L)F(L)F(L) for each 1≤L≤n1 \leq L \leq n1≤L≤n.

It can be shown that any F(L)F(L)F(L) can be expressed as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers, P≥0P \geq 0P≥0, Q>0Q > 0Q>0 and QQQ is coprime with 998244353998244353998244353. You should compute P×Q−1mod  998244353P \times Q^{-1} \mod 998244353P×Q−1mod998244353, where Q−1Q^{−1}Q−1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

First line will contain two integers, nnn (the length of string SSS) and KKK.
Next line will contain the string SSS.


Output Format
Print nnn space separated integers, F(L)F(L)F(L), for each 1≤L≤n1 \leq L \leq n1≤L≤n, in a single line.
constraints: It's guaranteed that S contains only lower-case Latin letters.
content.inputFormat: First line contains two integers, n (the length of string S) and K. Next line contains the string S.
content.outputFormat: Print n space separated integers, F(L), for each 1≤L≤n, in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In our sample, for L=3L= 3L=3, we have 3 sub-strings:

abaabaaba (Starting from 1st position)
babbabbab (Starting from 2nd position)
abaabaaba (Starting from 3rd position)

Among them we can choose in the following ways, to get all 3 of them pairwise identical:

(1,1,1):Probability = 13×13×13=127(1, 1, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,1,1):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(2,2,2):Probability = 13×13×13=127(2, 2, 2) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(2,2,2):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(3,3,3):Probability = 13×13×13=127(3, 3, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,3,3):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(1,1,3):Probability = 13×13×13=127(1, 1, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,1,3):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(1,3,1):Probability = 13×13×13=127(1, 3, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,3,1):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(3,1,1):Probability = 13×13×13=127(3, 1, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,1,1):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(3,3,1):Probability = 13×13×13=127(3, 3, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,3,1):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(3,1,3):Probability = 13×13×13=127(3, 1, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,1,3):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​
(1,3,3):Probability = 13×13×13=127(1, 3, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,3,3):Probability = 31​×31​×31​=271​

(1,3,3)(1, 3, 3)(1,3,3) means that the three random substrings chosen are the substring starting at index 1, at index 3 and at index 3.
So F(3)=9×127=13F(3) = 9 \times \frac{1}{27} = \frac{1}{3}F(3)=9×271​=31​

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will use dynamic programming and combinatorics. We will first calculate the number of ways to choose K substrings of length L from the string S. Then, we will calculate the probability of choosing all K substrings of length L pairwise identical. We will use the formula for combinations to calculate the number of ways to choose K substrings of length L from the string S. We will also use the formula for probability to calculate the probability of choosing all K substrings of length L pairwise identical. Finally, we will use the formula for modular arithmetic to calculate the final result.

code.Python3:
import sys
import math

MOD = 998244353

def pow(a, b, c):
    res = 1
    while b > 0:
        if (b & 1) == 1:
            res = (res * a) % c
        a = (a * a) % c
        b >>= 1
    return res

n, K = map(int, input().split())
S = input()

for l in range(1, n + 1):
    p = 1
    q = 1
    for i in range(n - l + 1):
        c = 0
        for j in range(K):
            if i + j * l < n:
                c += 1
        p = (p * c) % MOD
        q = (q * (c + 1)) % MOD
    r = (p * pow(q, MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
    s = (r * pow(q, MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
    t = (s * pow(q, MOD - 2, MOD)) % MOD
    print(t, end=' ')
print()
